meta.description: Defense UAS intelligence — DoD procurement, Blue UAS, Drone Dominance Program, NDAA, counter-UAS, tactical platforms, defense contractors

records.title: Drone Dominance Program Phase II — Accelerated Timeline, Camp Blanding Gauntlet II
records.summary: DDP Phase II strategy: accelerated RFS timeline, all-in pricing tiers confirmed, CLIK payload interface required, 8.5 GHz RF preference, multi-drone single-operator C2 scored Gauntlet II metric, KENETIK as preferred ESAD vendor. Three competitive plays: complete system entrant, avionics supplier to Phase I winners, Blue UAS Framework listing. Camp Blanding Industry Day intelligence incorporated. April 2026 acquisition timeline active.

records.title: Blue UAS Cleared List — Current Status and Procurement Pathway
records.summary: Defense Innovation Unit Blue UAS program: cleared list of NDAA-compliant drone platforms validated for DoD procurement. Blue UAS Select designation for platforms with additional cyber/performance assessment. Used by DoD buyers as approved list without routine exception-to-policy requests. Also referenced by state/local agencies for NDAA compliance. Key cleared platforms: Skydio X10D, BRINC Lemur 2, Parrot ANAFI USA, Inspired Flight IF800/IF1200A, Freefly Astro, ACSL SOTEN.

records.title: NDAA §848 + ASDA — Defense Procurement Restriction Timeline
records.summary: NDAA §848 (FY2020): DoD barred from Chinese-made drones and components. FY2024 NDAA/ASDA: expanded government-wide — federal agencies cannot procure covered UAS, operational ban Dec 22 2025. FY2025 NDAA §1709: DJI/Autel review-or-trigger → FCC Covered List Dec 23 2025. FY2026 NDAA: counter-UAS authority restructuring (§912 DoD task force, §3111 DOE nuclear sites, SAFER SKIES Act). SkyFoundry program: Army-led domestic drone production pipeline targeting DJI price advantage.

records.title: Executive Order — Unleashing American Drone Dominance (June 2025)
records.summary: Trump EO Unleashing American Drone Dominance (June 2025): FAA directed to publish NPRM for Part 108 within 30 days and finalize within 240 days. AI-based tools for waiver evaluation. Domestic drone production scale-up mandate. Pentagon pledged to approve hundreds of American-made drone models. Defense Secretary Hegseth: 30,000 attack drone production push. $1.4B funding initiative for domestic drone industrial base. SkyFoundry program concept: government-owned, contractor-operated production facilities.

records.title: Replicator Program — Status and Attritable UAS Scaling
records.summary: DoD Replicator initiative: fielding thousands of attritable autonomous systems across multiple domains within 18-24 months. Focused on small UAS, maritime, and land systems. Ukraine war lessons driving urgency — manufacturing volume over exotic capability. Replicator II expanding scope. Key vendors tracking: Anduril, Shield AI, Joby (logistics), Skydio, Red Cat, Teal Drones. Congress pushing SkyFoundry parallel track for price competition with DJI equivalents.

records.title: Silvus StreamCaster — Recommended DDP RF Play for 8.5 GHz and Multi-Drone C2
records.summary: Silvus StreamCaster recommended as stronger DDP play for Camp Blanding 8.5 GHz and multi-drone C2 scoring. Mobile Ad-hoc Network (MANET) architecture. StreamCaster 4200 operates 4.4–5.0 GHz and 8.0–9.0 GHz — covers DDP RF preference. Supports multi-drone single-operator C2 requirement (scored Gauntlet II metric). TrellisWare TW-950 (Barrage Relay Networks) better for SOF/EW-contested deep penetration. Persistent Systems MPU5 third option. Silvus vs TrellisWare differentiation: Silvus for Camp Blanding multi-drone scoring; TrellisWare for contested EMCON environments.

records.title: TReX II Defense Consortium — Orqa U.S. Subsidiary Eligibility Assessment
records.summary: TReX II (Tactical Robotics and Expeditionary Systems) DIU consortium: Orqa Inc. U.S. Delaware subsidiary could qualify with FOCI mitigation, DD2345, and SAM.gov registration. Charles Phillips (Orqa U.S.) identified as point of contact. Membership provides access to DoD procurement pathways, Blue UAS evaluation fast-track, and OTA contract vehicles. Key requirement: Foreign Ownership Control and Influence (FOCI) mitigation plan required for Croatian parent company.

records.title: FY2026 NDAA Counter-UAS Provisions — SAFER SKIES, §912 DoD Task Force, §3111 DOE
records.summary: FY2026 NDAA counter-UAS: §912 establishes dedicated DoD task force to unify C-UAS response — 180-day report to Congress on service-level authority differences. §3111 gives DOE explicit authority to detect, track, intercept, and destroy drones threatening nuclear sites. SAFER SKIES Act (Title LXXXVI): expands DHS/DOJ counter-UAS authority to state/local LE for sporting events and critical infrastructure. Strict reporting, oversight, civil liberties requirements. FIFA World Cup 2026 planning already incorporating these authorities.

records.title: Orqa MRM2-10 — US Army PM UAS Marketplace Intake Strategy
records.summary: Orqa MRM2-10 system intake form built for US Army PM UAS Marketplace. DDP acquisition pathway with accelerated April timeline. Blue List eligibility through COL Thomas at DCMA. Camp Blanding Gauntlet II strategy incorporating: CLIK payload interface, 8.5 GHz RF, multi-drone single-operator C2, KENETIK ESAD. Three simultaneous competitive plays: complete system entrant, avionics supplier to Phase I winners, Blue UAS Framework listing. Key contacts: Charles Phillips (U.S. ops), Brian Reed (manager), Petar (Orqa HQ).
